items.summary: The Office for National Statistics says some areas of the jobs market are weakening, as vacancies continue to fall.

items.title: Waterways Leisure Tourism IPO: Price band set at ₹769-808 per share; check key dates, issue details
items.summary: Waterways Leisure Tourism Limited sets its IPO price band between ₹769 to ₹808 per share, with subscription from June 23 to June 25. Reservations include 75% for QIBs, 15% for NIIs, and 10% for retail investors. Shares will list on BSE and NSE on July 1.

items.title: Apple to raise prices as AI boom pushes up chip costs
items.summary: The firm's outgoing boss Tim Cook did not say when prices will rise or which products will be affected.

items.title: Oil falls as International Energy Agency forecasts supply glut next year after U.S.-Iran deal
items.summary: Oil declines as Mideast peace prospects rise following U.S.-Iran deal.

items.summary: NSE's IPO is likely to be worth $3.3 billion, based on its share price in private markets, and comes after years of regulatory delays. Existing investors will sell 6% of the company's equity as part of the issue, which will be a pure offer-for-sale with no fresh equity being raised.

items.title: BSE among 5 F&O stocks with a sharp rise in futures open interest
items.summary: Five NSE F&O stocks saw futures open interest jump over 11% on June 17, led by Nuvama Wealth Management, indicating heightened trader participation, fresh position creation, and growing market interest.

items.title: US Stock Market: Warsh signals overhaul of policy communications, skips rate projection in major shift
items.summary: Federal Reserve Chair Kevin Warsh signalled a possible overhaul of the US central bank’s communication strategy after declining to submit his own interest-rate forecast in the latest SEP. As policymakers shift towards potential rate hikes amid persistent inflation, the Fed is reviewing key policy tools while maintaining confidence in labour market resilience.

items.title: Vedanta Power shares drop 3%, fall below listing price. What lies ahead?
items.summary: Vedanta Power shares fell over 3% on Thursday, slipping below their listing price after debuting earlier this week following the Vedanta demerger. Analysts said the stock remains the most defensive among the four newly listed entities, supported by regulated returns, while advising investors to focus on business quality, sector cycles and execution outlook.

items.title: India-UK FTA optimism lifts Gokaldas, Radico Khaitan, Avanti Feeds shares
items.summary: Investor sentiment was buoyed by the prospect of lower import duties and enhanced market access under the trade pact, which is expected to benefit companies across textiles, footwear, marine exports and alcoholic beverages.

items.title: AI-led growth continues to draw global capital into the US: Mitul Kotecha
items.summary: The Federal Reserve's hawkish stance has surprised markets, with revised inflation forecasts and the dot plot indicating a commitment to higher rates for longer. While inflation remains elevated, potential easing in early 2025 hinges on moderating price pressures.

items.title: A year in, Cyient CEO reshapes leadership to revive growth
items.summary: CEO Sukamal Banerjee has brought in at least eight senior executives as the family-led IT firm seeks larger deals, better margins and a turnaround after falling behind peers Coforge and Persistent.

items.title: Sensex today | Stock Market Live: Sensex drops over 100 pts, Nifty holds above 24,000 as oil price relief offsets Fed rate concerns
items.summary: Sensex, Nifty, Share Prices LIVE: Indian markets trade flat as investors balanced lower crude oil prices and US Federal Reserve signals. While softer oil prices supported sentiment by easing inflation concerns, expectations of future US rate hikes kept risk appetite cautious.

items.title: Nykaa shares jump 7% as FY30 roadmap impresses Street
items.summary: Nykaa expects FY30 growth to be driven by higher spending on beauty and fashion, expansion of its store network, and continued gains in its online and owned-brand businesses.

items.title: NSE appoints record 20 merchant bankers for ₹30,000 crore IPO; mega issue to be entirely OFS
items.summary: The offer will be an Offer for Sale, with existing shareholders selling shares, while NSE targets one of India’s largest public listings.

items.title: Japan's Asahi taps Varun Beverages to make India debut with 'CALPIS'
items.summary: Japan's Asahi Group is bringing its CALPIS brand to India through a partnership with Varun Beverages Limited. This marks Asahi's entry into India's non-alcoholic, non-carbonated beverage market. Varun Beverages will handle manufacturing, distribution, and sales. CALPIS is expected to launch in India by the second half of 2026. The brand will offer dairy-based drinks in Original and Mango flavors.

items.title: Kirloskar Ferrous share price jumps 14%, hits 52-week high. What's driving the rally?
items.summary: Kirloskar Ferrous shares opened at ₹467 apiece today, as compared to ₹444.30 on Wednesday. The stock touched an intraday high of ₹508.45 on 18 June.

items.title: NSE IPO Effect: New India Assurance shares rally 14% on plans to offload over 1 crore shares in blockbuster offer
items.summary: New India Assurance shares surged over 14% following the National Stock Exchange's (NSE) filing of its Draft Red Herring Prospectus (DRHP) for its long-awaited Initial Public Offering (IPO). The government-owned insurer will offload over 1 crore shares as part of the offer-for-sale, with seven PSU shareholders divesting nearly 8 crore shares in total.

items.title: Is BPCL undervalued? The stock's valuation tells an interesting story
items.summary: BPCL has strengthened its balance sheet, expanded into green hydrogen and posted robust profits. But do its current valuations fully reflect those gains?

items.title: RIL AGM 2026: Jio IPO and 4 other big bets Mukesh Ambani could unveil this Friday
items.summary: Reliance Industries Chairman Mukesh Ambani is set to reveal major plans at the 49th annual general meeting. Investors anticipate updates on the Jio IPO, a significant $110 billion AI and data center blueprint, and progress on new energy giga complexes. Retail expansion and updates on the core Oil-to-Chemicals segment are also expected.

items.title: Defence stocks extend rally; BEL, HAL, Bharat Dynamics, Zen Technologies among top stocks to buy
items.summary: Defence stocks to buy: Aequs and Zen Technologies climbed more than 5% each, being the top gainers on the Nifty India Defence index. Other gainers included Dynamatic Technologies, Solar Industries India, Astra Microwave Products, and MTAR Technologies, which rose between 1% and 3%.

items.title: Vedanta Aluminium vs Power vs Oil & Gas vs Iron & Steel: Which stock should you buy?
items.summary: Four Vedanta Group companies debuted on the market, with aluminium, iron & steel, and oil & gas shares tumbling while power stocks rose. Analysts advise patience, focusing on business quality over price action. Experts suggest aluminium offers the best risk-reward for long-term investors due to strong demand and cost efficiencies.

items.title: At 52-week high! YES Bank share price jumps 16% in 5 sessions: Should you buy? Here's what technicals suggest
items.summary: YES Bank shares reached a 52-week high of ₹25.77, marking a 16% rally over five sessions. The stock's bullish momentum follows a strategic partnership and recent RBI measures, with predictions for further gains as technical indicators remain positive.

items.title: Tata Motors raises commercial vehicle prices after hiking passenger vehicle rates
items.summary: Tata Motors is raising prices for its commercial vehicles. The increase will be up to 2.5 percent starting July 1. This move follows a similar price hike for passenger vehicles. The company aims to manage higher commodity and input costs. This decision impacts Tata Motors' entire domestic vehicle range. Consumers will bear some of the increased expenses.

items.title: US Stock Market: Trump backs Fed Chair Warsh after rate hold, softens tone on monetary policy
items.summary: US President Donald Trump expressed confidence in Federal Reserve Chairman Kevin Warsh, a departure from his past criticisms of the central bank. While the Fed kept rates steady, with some policymakers hinting at future hikes, Trump indicated he trusts Warsh's judgment. Warsh, meanwhile, maintained a cautious stance on future rate movements and confirmed regular contact with the Treasury Secretary.

items.title: FILA Group sells 7% stake in DOMS Industries for Rs 934 crore
items.summary: Italian multinational FILA Group has sold a 7 percent stake in DOMS Industries for Rs 934 crore. SBI Mutual Fund and Axis Mutual Fund have bought a combined 2.13 percent stake for Rs 285 crore. FILA Group's shareholding in DOMS Industries has reduced. DOMS Industries manufactures stationery and art materials. The company's shares saw a rise after the transactions.

items.title: India taps World Bank, ADB for $2.5 billion infrastructure spend
items.summary: India is in talks with the World Bank and Asian Development Bank for substantial funding. This financial support aims to boost urban infrastructure and create jobs. The loans will help New Delhi manage its budget after increased spending on subsidies. The government plans to upgrade infrastructure and urban renewal projects.

items.title: KKR eyes at least $1 billion stake in Medicover's India hospital arm, source says
items.summary: Global investment firm KKR is in advanced discussions to acquire a significant stake in Medicover's Indian hospital operations. The deal is valued at over one billion dollars. Medicover, a Swedish healthcare group, confirmed these talks. This potential acquisition marks a substantial expansion for KKR in India's growing healthcare sector.

items.title: NSE vs BSE: Should investors buy BSE shares now or wait for one of the biggest IPOs in India?
items.summary: NSE's DRHP filing brings its IPO closer, but analysts suggest BSE's stock has already priced in this event. While the listing offers a direct comparison, BSE's future upside is expected to be driven by its own business performance and earnings growth, not solely by the NSE IPO trigger.

items.title: Market has already priced in plenty of negativity; outlook looks promising: Prashant Khemka
items.summary: Prashant Khemka of WhiteOak Group believes the Indian equity market has largely priced in current pessimism, arguing that uncertainty is a constant in investing. He notes that a significant adjustment has already occurred, making it a favorable time for investors. Khemka also dismisses concerns of a market bubble in India.

items.title: From Rs 2 crore to Rs 5,000 crore! How NSE IPO is a jackpot for SBI with 256,775% profit
items.summary: State Bank of India anticipates a massive Rs 5,000 crore profit from the National Stock Exchange's upcoming Rs 30,000 crore IPO. This IPO is set to be India's largest ever. Many long-term investors are also poised for extraordinary returns on their initial investments. The NSE's listing on the BSE follows years of anticipation and regulatory hurdles.

items.title: HFCL shares soar after securing ₹2,666 cr BharatNet Phase-III contract from RVNL
items.summary: The stock rose as much as 4.6 per cent to ₹198.90 on the NSE after the company announced that it had secured a major contract from Rail Vikas Nigam Ltd (RVNL) for the BharatNet Phase-III project.

items.title: US Stock Market: Analysts see inflation driving Fed's hawkish shift under Warsh
items.summary: The Federal Reserve held interest rates steady, signaling potential future hikes due to persistent inflation concerns. Policymakers' updated projections now show fewer rate cuts expected next year, reflecting a hawkish shift. Markets reacted cautiously, with equities dipping and Treasury yields rising as investors anticipate prolonged higher rates.

items.title: HFCL shares jump 5% after Rs 2,666-crore RVNL order; stock soars 200% in 6 months
items.summary: HFCL shares surged after securing a significant contract from Rail Vikas Nigam Limited for the BharatNet project. This order strengthens HFCL's telecom network presence. The company is also expanding into defence and aerospace sectors. HFCL is a key player in optical fibre cable manufacturing and is involved in AI connectivity. Its export revenue is projected to grow substantially.

items.title: US Fed's hawkish tone shuts door on India bond rally for now
items.summary: The yield on the benchmark 6.94% 2036 bond rose to 6.8639% by 10:20 ‌a.m. IST from its previous close of 6.8626%.

items.title: The onshore gateway to global capital
items.summary: India's economy is set to grow significantly, but its global market presence lags. GIFT IFSC is building infrastructure to bridge this gap. Indian households can now invest in global markets through a regulated route. This offers tax and regulatory advantages. The platform facilitates easy access to international exchanges for retail investors. This development marks a structural shift in capital flows.

items.title: Defence stocks fire up! Paras Defence, BEL, GRSE, other stocks surge up to 24% in 2 days. What's powering the rally?
items.summary: Defence stocks extended their rally for a second straight session, with Paras Defence surging 24% in two days and BEL, GRSE, HAL, and others posting strong gains. Investor sentiment was boosted by record FY26 defence production, rising private-sector participation, growing exports, and optimism around India's self-reliance push.

items.title: Why Tata Motors and Ashok Leyland stocks are racing ahead of the auto pack
items.summary: A sharp drop in crude oil prices is easing pressure on fleet operators and reviving sentiment around commercial vehicle makers. But are Tata Motors and Ashok Leyland out of the woods yet?

items.title: NSE IPO: SBI, Bank of Baroda among top sellers in OFS, set for multibagger gains; LIC stays put
items.summary: NSE IPO: State Bank of India (SBI) is the largest shareholder participating in the OFS of NSE IPO. SBI will sell up to 2.48 crore NSE shares. According to the DRHP, SBI’s weighted average acquisition cost for these shares is merely ₹0.80 per share.

items.title: Vedanta Aluminium share price gains for the first time since listing; Kotak, Citi initiate buy calls
items.summary: Vedanta Aluminium shares rose 3% on June 18 after two brokerages initiated coverage with Buy ratings, signaling positive growth prospects driven by capacity expansions and cost efficiency. The stock debuted at ₹522, a significant premium over its initial price.

items.title: NSE IPO to create significant value for investors; Tata Motors a strong long-term bet: Dipan Mehta
items.summary: Dipan Mehta of Elixir Equities views the National Stock Exchange's IPO as a significant event for India's capital markets, promising greater transparency and wealth unlocking for investors. He also advises caution on the automobile sector due to multiple headwinds but remains optimistic about Tata Motors' long-term prospects, citing its product pipeline and EV strategy.

items.title: Sensex, Nifty cautious despite softer crude; IT shares slide after hawkish US Fed commentary
items.summary: Nifty IT index declines over 1.5%

items.title: ET Alpha Wealth Summit | Is alpha dead in Indian markets? Devina Mehra says no, but the game has changed
items.summary: Stock-picking is not over, but the way to achieve better returns is changing. Most investors are missing the real story. Devina Mehra of First Global highlighted that a narrow rally last year meant many portfolios suffered even as indices rose. She stressed that diversification is mathematical, not magical, and global investing is key.

items.title: Will Zee Entertainment’s FIFA World Cup deal turn the needle for its 3.3 crore shareholders?
items.summary: Zee Entertainment Enterprises pulled off a surprise win by securing India's broadcast and digital rights for multiple FIFA events through 2034, including the FIFA World Cups in 2026 and 2030, after rivals failed to clinch the deal. The move has boosted investor optimism, with Zee shares gaining over 30% in the past month.

items.title: Stocks to buy or sell: Osho Krishan of Angel One suggests buying Bank of Baroda, Siemens Energy shares on 18 June
items.summary: Indian equities opened flat as lower crude oil prices offset weakness in IT stocks amid Fed's interest rate remarks. Nifty 50 rose 0.11% while Sensex increased 0.12%. The market shows bullish momentum with positive breadth, and key support levels identified for maintaining the uptrend.

items.title: HFCL share price rises 4% after bagging order from RVNL. Do you own?
items.summary: HFCL share price opened at ₹197 apiece today, as compared to previous close of ₹190 on Wednesday. The stock touched an intraday high of ₹199 per share on 18 June.

items.summary: At 10.03 am on Thursday, August Brent oil futures were at $77.94, down by 2.02 per cent, and July crude oil futures on WTI (West Texas Intermediate) were at $75, down by 2.33 per cent

items.summary: Foreign governments, Wall Street banks and multinational companies are flocking to China's panda bond market.

items.summary: Union Minister Suresh Gopi explained that immediate fuel price reductions aren't possible despite global crude cost drops due to transit times and other factors. He highlighted the central government's Rs 12,000 crore absorption of past impacts, urging states to also contribute.

items.summary: Life Insurance Corporation of India will not sell its stake in the National Stock Exchange's IPO. LIC holds a significant 10.7% stake in NSE. The insurer believes NSE has long-term value creation potential. This decision aligns with LIC's strategy of building substantial stakes in major Indian companies. NSE's growth and market dominance support LIC's long-term investment view.

items.summary: The National Stock Exchange of India filed its Draft Red Herring Prospectus for a proposed ₹30,000 crore IPO, potentially the largest in Indian history, surpassing Hyundai Motor India's previous record. The offering consists entirely of an offer-for-sale with no fresh capital for the exchange.

items.title: NSE’s IPO may deliver 6,400-fold return for early backers
items.summary: NSE’s upcoming IPO is set to generate extraordinary returns for early investors, with some public sector and institutional backers reporting gains of up to 6,400 times. SBI, Temasek, and Morgan Stanley are among key beneficiaries as long-held stakes in the exchange unlock massive value amid India’s capital market expansion.

items.title: NSE IPO: NIACL, IFCI, other stocks gain up to 14% as NSE files for India's largest IPO. Who's selling stake?
items.summary: Shares of IFCI, SBI, and other companies saw significant gains on Thursday. This surge followed the National Stock Exchange filing its draft IPO papers. Several companies are named as selling shareholders in the Offer for Sale component. The NSE's public issue is anticipated to be India's largest.

items.title: Restoring India’s land, securing India’s future through science and collective action
items.summary: Scientific innovations and integrated farming systems are combating land degradation and enhancing climate resilience, benefiting millions of farmers and contributing to national development goals.

items.summary: Gold and silver prices fell sharply on MCX after the US Federal Reserve signalled a possible interest rate hike later this year while keeping rates unchanged. Higher rate expectations pressured precious metals despite easing oil prices. Analysts advised booking profits in existing long positions and waiting for fresh entry levels.

items.title: NSE IPO back on track as exchange files prospectus with SEBI for ₹30,000-crore issue
items.summary: Pure offer-for-sale of up to 149 million equity shares likely to be one of India’s biggest public issues of the year

items.title: Sensex trades flat, Nifty below 24,050 as markets snap gaining streak after Fed’s hawkish signal
items.summary: Indian stock market is trading lower as Sensex and Nifty snap a four-session gaining streak after a hawkish US Federal Reserve tone raised rate hike expectations. IT stocks lead losses, while broader markets remain resilient. India VIX eases and market breadth stays positive despite weak benchmark performance.

items.title: ET Alpha Wealth Summit | Wealthy Indians are quietly shifting money into gold, global stocks and AI infrastructure; Rajesh Saluja explains why
items.summary: Indian investors are shifting focus from maximizing returns to building resilient portfolios. Experts advise diversification across assets and geographies. Gold is gaining prominence as an inflation hedge. The focus is on yield-generating assets like REITs and private credit. Opportunities are emerging in AI infrastructure, particularly data centers, supported by India's renewable energy growth.

items.title: PM Modi and Trump direct officials to fast-track 'commercially meaningful' trade pact
items.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi and US President Donald Trump agreed to push for an early conclusion of a balanced and commercially meaningful bilateral trade agreement during talks on the sidelines of the G7 Summit in France. The two leaders reviewed progress under the India-US COMPACT initiative and welcomed advances in defence, technology, energy and trade cooperation.

items.title: First signs of LNG traffic return emerge at Strait of Hormuz with a relief for Pakistan
items.summary: An LNG tanker loaded in Qatar is approaching the Strait of Hormuz, signalling a possible revival in traffic after a temporary US-Iran peace deal. The vessel, Mraikh, has been stranded in the Persian Gulf since February and is now headed toward Pakistan. The move comes as Trump announced a deal to reopen Hormuz, whose closure had disrupted nearly 20% of global LNG supplies.

items.title: Gold, Silver rates today: MCX gold rate slips below ₹1.53 lakh per 10 grams, silver price crashes by ₹6,000 per kg
items.summary: Gold, Silver rates today: Gold prices in the international market recouped losses from the previous session, as a drop in oil prices following a US-Iran interim agreement dampened inflation expectations and supported bullion.

items.title: BSE shares in focus as rival NSE files DRHP with Sebi for mega IPO
items.summary: BSE shares are under scrutiny as rival NSE files for India's largest IPO. While some see this as a natural reaction, experts believe it offers a direct valuation benchmark. The NSE's IPO, an offer-for-sale by several public sector entities, is expected to list on BSE itself. Investors anticipate a return to fundamental analysis post-listing.

items.title: Vedanta Aluminium shares jump over 3% after Citi, Kotak initiate with Buy, see up to 29% upside. Here’s why
items.summary: Vedanta Aluminium Metal shares surged over 3% after Citi and Kotak Institutional Equities initiated coverage with 'Buy' ratings and target prices of Rs 560 and Rs 600, respectively. Analysts cited a positive aluminium outlook, growth potential, cost efficiencies, and improving leverage as key drivers for their bullish calls.

items.title: Rupee opens 13 paise lower at 94.66 against US dollar
items.summary: The Indian rupee opened at 94.66 against the US dollar, weakened by a hawkish Fed stance. Goldman Sachs raised inflation forecasts, predicting one rate hike in 2026. The rupee later strengthened to 95.08, buoyed by geopolitical sentiment and foreign capital inflows into India’s debt market.

items.title: NSE IPO: 7 PSU shareholders to offload 8 crore shares in mega public offer
items.summary: Seven government-owned entities, including State Bank of India and Bank of Baroda, will sell stakes in the National Stock Exchange through its upcoming IPO. This move brings the NSE closer to its listing, anticipated to be India's largest public offering. The exchange, a global leader in equity derivatives, has shown strong financial performance.

items.title: Gold climbs over 1% as oil drops on US-Iran interim deal
items.summary: Oil prices fell, reversing gains made ⁠on Wednesday after Trump said he could resume his bombing campaign if Iran's leaders "don't behave"

items.title: Bitcoin price falls below $65,000: These two major global events are spooking crypto investors
items.summary: Bitcoin continues to face market pressure due to geopolitical and monetary policy concerns. The US-Iran interim agreement extends the current ceasefire by 60 days, permitting toll-free oil transport through the Strait of Hormuz while addressing unresolved nuclear and economic issues.

items.title: Google Gemini co-lead Noam Shazeer leaves for OpenAI
items.summary: Google's vice president of engineering and a co-leader of its Gemini AI models announced Wednesday he is leaving the company to join OpenAI.

items.title: Buy or sell: Gift Nifty flat, Vaishali Parekh recommends three stocks to buy as Trump signs US-Iran deal
items.summary: Buy or sell: Vaishali Parekh recommends three stocks to buy today —BEL, NALCO, and IRFC

items.title: RBI not in favour of offshore settlement for sovereign bonds: Report
items.summary: India's central bank, the RBI, is opting against direct settlement of government securities via offshore platforms like Euroclear. Instead, it prefers overseas investors to trade directly on the domestic NDS-OM platform. This move aims to consolidate liquidity and enhance price discovery, despite recent tax incentives designed to attract foreign capital.

items.title: Siemens Energy among 3 stocks flash bullish signals, indicating possible uptrend
items.summary: Three NSE largecap stocks, including Siemens Energy India, Trent, and Hindustan Aeronautics, showed bullish momentum on April 27 by forming a White Marubozu pattern, according to StockEdge data. items.title: Why a strong El Niño leaves tropical commodities acutely exposed
items.summary: Forecasters anticipate a strong El Nino developing later this year, likely boosting global temperatures and disrupting rainfall patterns. This poses significant risks to soft commodities like cocoa, coffee, and sugar, particularly those grown in tropical regions. Past El Nino events have historically led to reduced yields and price surges for these crops.

items.title: States told to prepare contingency plans for districts likely to face low rainfall during kharif season
items.summary: India faces its weakest monsoon in 11 years. Agriculture Minister Shivraj Singh Chouhan has instructed states to prepare contingency plans for districts likely to experience low or uneven rainfall this kharif season. The government is focusing on water conservation and alternative cropping patterns. Efforts are also underway to boost cotton production through scientific farming.

items.title: CAG asks states to disclose off-budget borrowings in A/Cs
items.summary: India's Comptroller and Auditor General, K Sanjay Murthy, urged state governments to fully disclose their off-budget borrowings. He highlighted that these liabilities have reached ₹90.51 lakh crore. States are advised to reduce these debts over time. Accurate reporting is crucial for understanding the true scale of state finances and ensuring fiscal transparency. This will aid informed decision-making by policymakers.

items.title: Finmin to engage with states over looming El Nino fallout

This review will focus on potential stress on agriculture, food inflation, and increased subsidy outgo. The government is preparing relief packages for vulnerable districts. This proactive approach aims to ensure adequate supplies and mitigate inflation spikes.

Overall, listed private non-financial companies achieved double-digit sales growth. Raw material expenses increased, indicating input cost pressure. Staff costs for manufacturing companies moderated. Services sector companies experienced a moderation in operating profit margins.

items.title: States' expenditure rises 131% in 10 yrs on welfare, development: CAG Report
items.summary: States' spending surged 131 percent from 2015-16 to 2024-25. Welfare and development drove this growth. Revenue expenditure remained the largest budget component. Committed expenses and subsidies consumed over half of revenue spending. Total state expenditure reached Rs 51.20 lakh crore in 2024-25. Budgetary spending averaged between 15.78 and 17.49 percent of GSDP.

items.title: India's rice stocks climb to record high; wheat inventories at five-year peak
items.summary: India's government warehouses hold record rice stocks, up 15% from last year. Wheat inventories are at a five-year peak. These abundant reserves will support increased rice exports. Higher wheat stocks will help manage prices by allowing open market sales. Record production in the last crop year fueled these impressive inventories. India is well-positioned for both exports and domestic price stability.

Improved rainfall is anticipated from June 18 onwards in some regions. A heatwave is also expected in Vidarbha.
